title: Warhammer Quest - Let’s Play - Part 4
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=14FLm6zvvlM

Daniel (@dungeondive) rebuilds the dungeon for a second attempt at the Dragon's Eye quest. The warriors' backstory takes shape thanks to a viewer suggestion: young Harlan was rescued from a chaos cult by Saul the Witch Hunter, and now serves as his indebted companion. It's a neat narrative hook that gives their partnership real weight.

The second dungeon starts in the Circle of Power and quickly delivers a proper fight—four skeletons that require fear checks before the warriors can engage. Harlan goes berserk for the first time, earning two attacks per turn and carving through enemies with satisfying deathblows. The heroes discover a dying orc clutching a mysterious iron key, a cellar trapdoor, and navigate through a labyrinth hall with three branching paths. The dungeon layout is more complex this time, with Daniel showcasing fan-made tiles from the Beyond the Great Mountains and Hall of the Hag Queen expansions. The episode ends on a cliffhanger as the warriors prepare to explore deeper—still hunting for the fire chasm objective room.

----

What's your favorite fan-made or DIY expansion content for Warhammer Quest? Any hidden gems the community should know about?
